Title: The OsNAC24-OsNAP protein complex activates OsGBSSI and OsSBEI expression to fine-tune starch biosynthesis in rice endosperm

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAC transcription factor; Va(1)/NAP subfamily; conserved N-terminal NAC domain with subdomains A-E
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ubiquitous, highest in developing seeds, especially endosperm; peaks at 7 DAF; also higher in seed coat than embryo and hull at 7 DAF
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in developing endosperm at 5-10 DAF, peaking at 7 DAF
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major storage carbohydrate in rice endosperm; key determinant of grain quality, cooking and eating quality
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Developmental program of seed/endosperm filling; OsNAP-mediated nuclear translocation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Starch biosynthesis, including amylose synthesis and amylopectin branch structure formation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; direct promoter binding; coactivation via protein complex with OsNAP; nuclear translocation-enhanced activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates OsGBSSI and OsSBEI strongly; also directly targets OsAGPS2, OsSSI, OsSSIIIa and OsSSIVb; in mutants OsGBSSI and OsSBEI mRNA/protein decrease
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: OsGBSSI; OsSBEI; OsAGPS2; OsSSI; OsSSIIIa; OsSSIVb
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; overexpression; qRT-PCR; Western blot; ChIP-seq; ChIP-PCR; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; subcellular localization; starch/metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing endosperm during grain filling under normal field/greenhouse growth; key stages 3-25 DAF, especially 7 DAF
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of function reduces total starch and amylose, alters amylopectin chain-length distribution and starch physicochemical properties without obvious endosperm morphology defects; overexpression increases amylose and causes chalky endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Fine-tuning regulator of endosperm starch synthesis mainly through activation of OsGBSSI and OsSBEI
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: ChIP-seq (accession not reported); gene expression database screening mentioned without accession
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression causes chalky endosperm and altered starch granule morphology; knockout changes starch composition and quality traits
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to fine-tune amylose content and eating/cooking quality by editing regulator or its binding motifs in target promoters; caution that overexpression may induce chalkiness
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Binds newly identified motifs TTGACAA, AGAAGA and ACAAGA in addition to core NAC motif CACG; direct activation of some targets in protoplasts does not fully match mutant transcript changes for OsSSI and OsSSIVb
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNAC24 is a NAC transcriptional activator highly expressed in rice endosperm and peaking at 7 DAF. 2. CRISPR knockout reduces total starch and amylose and alters amylopectin chain distribution, mainly associated with decreased OsGBSSI and OsSBEI transcript and protein abundance. 3. OsNAC24 directly binds promoters of six starch enzyme-coding genes and preferentially recognizes TTGACAA, AGAAGA/ACAAGA, and CACG motifs. 4. OsNAP interacts with OsNAC24 to enhance nuclear localization and coactivate target genes, especially OsGBSSI.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAC transcription factor; Va(1)/NAP subfamily; contains functional NLS peptide VTGKRKRSSD
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional role analyzed in developing endosperm from 3-25 DAF
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major storage carbohydrate in rice endosperm; key determinant of grain quality
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Starch biosynthesis and expression of starch-synthesis enzyme-coding genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional coactivation with OsNAC24; promotes nuclear translocation of OsNAC24; also activates OsGBSSI promoter in protoplast assay
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes OsGBSSI transcription; loss of function alters expression of all tested SECGs and further lowers OsGBSSI/OsSBEI in osnac24 osnap double mutants
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: OsGBSSI; OsSBEI; starch-synthesis enzyme-coding genes tested in endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; subcellular localization; dual-luciferase assay; qRT-PCR; starch profiling; double-mutant analysis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing endosperm during grain filling under normal growth conditions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of function reduces total starch and increases soluble sugars without obvious change in amylose or endosperm appearance; enhances OsNAC24-dependent activation of OsGBSSI
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Co-regulator that boosts OsNAC24 function through protein interaction and nuclear relocalization, strongly amplifying OsGBSSI expression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation reduces total starch; combinatorial loss with OsNAC24 further decreases starch traits
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential combinatorial engineering target with OsNAC24 to tune starch content/quality; caution needed because reduced function lowers starch accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Does not alter OsNAC24 transcript level in osnap mutants, indicating action is post-transcriptional through localization/complex formation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNAP physically interacts with OsNAC24 and localizes in the nucleus. 2. OsNAP promotes nuclear accumulation of OsNAC24 and massively enhances OsNAC24-dependent activation of the OsGBSSI promoter. 3. CRISPR osnap mutants show reduced total starch and increased soluble sugars, with altered expression of starch-synthesis genes. 4. Double mutants with osnac24 show stronger reductions in OsGBSSI/OsSBEI expression and starch traits, confirming cooperative regulation.
